content:
{
  "gto_principles": "GTO (Game Theory Optimal) aims for unexploitable play by balancing ranges and sizings. For bots, GTO provides a robust baseline but sacrifices EV vs weak opponents. Key: use mixed strategies (e.g., check-raise some % of time) and range-wide frequencies. Modern solvers (PioSolver, GTO+) show GTO requires varying bet sizes based on board texture and stack depth.",
  "exploitation_triggers": "Switch from GTO to exploitative when opponent deviations are statistically significant (e.g., >5% fold to c-bet deviation). Triggers: (a) high fold-to-3bet → tighten 3bet range; (b) low fold-to-cbet → value-bet thin/check more; (c) not folding to raises → stop bluffing. Exploit: polarized sizing vs nits, small bets vs stations. Revert to GTO when opponent adjusts or after a bluff gets caught.",
  "icm_adjustments": "In tournaments, ICM pressure increases near the bubble and final table. (a) Shorter stacks: push/fold tighter than chip EV (fold marginal hands vs big stacks). (b) Big stacks: pressure medium stacks with wide raises but avoid flipping for huge pots. (c) Bubble: tighten calling ranges vs shoves; widen shoving ranges for small stacks (steal blinds with any two under 10bb). (d) Final table pay jumps: adjust push/fold with explicit ICM calculator integration.",
  "preflop_ranges_summary": "Based on 100bb deep GTO: (a) UTG: 12-15% of hands, pairs 22+, AJo+, ATs+, KQo, KTs+, QJs+. (b) MP: 18-22%, add ATo, KJo, QJo, suited connectors down to 54s. (c) CO: 25-30%, add all suited Aces, offsuit broadways, 64s+. (d) BTN: 40-50%, many suited cards, all pairs, offsuit connectors (J8o etc.). SB: tighter (call/3bet), BB defends wide (calls vs minraise ~60%). Ranges adjust by stack: <20bb push/fold; <40bb use small opens.",
  "postflop_bet_sizing": "1/3 pot: thin value, merging range, vs capped ranges (e.g., BTN vs BB on low boards). 1/2 pot: standard c-bet on dry boards; good for value and bluffs with equity. 2/3 pot: polarizing on medium/wet boards (turn/river). Pot bet: strong value or large bluffs vs weak ranges; common on river with nuts. Overbet (1.5-2x pot): polarizing on turns/rivers with extreme advantage (e.g., flush completing), forces opponent to overfold. Modern solver: use multiple sizings per street, not fixed.",
  "opponent_archetypes": "Nits: fold to aggression, steal blinds wide, value-bet thin, reduce bluff frequency. Fish (loose-passive): bet big for value, check-raise only with good hands, overbet draws. Calling stations: no bluff, value-bet all streets, use pot control with marginal hands, exploit with thin value. LAGs (loose-aggressive): 4-bet wider, re-raise with blockers, call flop c-bets more, trap with strong hands, use check-raises liberally.",
  "push_fold_thresholds": "Nash equilibrium for heads-up (bb ante assumed): <10bb push any two from SB; <8bb call range vs SB push: top 50% (22+, Ax, Kx, Q5+, J8+, 98s+). For multi-way (full ring ICM-adjusted): <12bb push from late position with any ace, any pair, suited connectors. <15bb tighten in early position. Deep stacks (>20bb) avoid pure push/fold; use minraise and 3bet shoves with 15-20bb.",
  "board_texture_rules": "Dry boards (e.g., K72 rainbow): high c-bet frequency (65-75%), small bets (1/3-1/2). Wet boards (e.g., 876 with flush draw): low c-bet (35-45%), larger bets (2/3+). Paired boards (e.g., 55A): check behind often, small bets with marginal hands. Dynamic boards (e.g., 2-tone connected): use mixed check/c-bet, check-raise with draws. Modern solver: c-bet size correlates with board connectivity and range advantage.",
  "meta_game_tips": "Adaptation speed: update opponent models every 5-10 hands within a match. Use sliding window (last 30-50 hands) for stat reliability. Switch from GTO to exploitative after detecting pattern with 90% confidence interval. Include a 'revert to GTO' trigger when opponent adjusts (e.g., if fold-to-cbet drops >10% after you increased bluffs). Use machine learning to classify opponent archetypes online. In multi-table tournaments, scale adaptation by opponent stack and recent actions (e.g., tight on bubble)."
}
